$comment: C99/C23 <tgmath.h> (7.25) — FC17.9(g) (D-CSUBSET-TGMATH). Type-generic math over the 17 shipped math.json functions, PURE CONFIG (zero src/ change): each name is a function-like `_Generic` macro (the stdbit.json macro precedent) dispatching float→f-variant / everything-else→the f64 function. STANDALONE: a TU may include ONLY <tgmath.h>, so this descriptor ships BOTH the macros AND its own symbol rows (duplicate rows vs math.json are first-wins-safe — the size_t ×6 / memcpy ×2 precedent); library map = math.json's (elf libm.so.6 per D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED). ★ THE CAST DISCIPLINE (both directions load-bearing, empirically proven): (1) the float arm MUST carry the explicit `(float)` cast — `_Generic`'s UNSELECTED arms are fully type-checked, so a naked `sqrtf((x))` arm rejects a legal f64 call via the arm's F64→F32 narrowing (S_TypeMismatch); (2) the default arm MUST stay BARE `sqrt((x))` — NEVER `(double)(x)`: that cast LAUNDERS a `_Complex` arg ((double)z is legal C, silently drops the imaginary part → a conformance MISCOMPILE) and launders walled long-double values at the semantic tier. Bare ⇒ a complex arg fails S_TypeMismatch LOUD (D-CSUBSET-TGMATH-COMPLEX — the complex tgmath surface rides D-CSUBSET-COMPLEX-TRANSCENDENTAL) and an F80/F128 long-double arg stays LOUD on the walled formats (rides the D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE value arc). No `double:`/`long double:` arms exist AT ALL (float + default only), so the f64-format long-double≡double intern collapse (D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-GENERIC-DISTINCT) is structurally routed around — the stdbit width-routing precedent; integer args ride `default:` via int→f64 implicit widening (C 7.25p3: any non-float real arg → the double version). The self-reference (`default: sqrt((x))` inside `#define sqrt(x)`) is Prosser-blue-painted (no re-expansion) and binds this descriptor's own extern row. TWO-ARG (pow/atan2/fmod): nested `_Generic` — BOTH args float → the f-variant with `(float)` casts on both; ANY other combo → the bare f64 fn. ldexp dispatches on arg 1 only (arg 2 is int). ★ PE GAP (D-CSUBSET-MATH-FLOAT-VARIANTS-PE): msvcrt.dll does NOT export fabsf/ldexpf (loader fails 0xC0000139 — DSS imports EVERY declared shipped extern, referenced or not), so those 2 symbol rows are gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho] and the fabs/ldexp MACROS carry per-format `variants` (the setjmp.json format-keyed precedent) whose pe float arm routes THROUGH the f64 function with an EXACT round-trip: |x| and 2^n·x of any F32 are exactly representable in F64, so `(float)fabs((double)(x))` / `(float)ldexp((double)(x),(y))` return bit-identical results to fabsf/ldexpf (a 2^n·x that overflows F32 rounds/overflows at the final `(float)` narrow exactly as ldexpf would). The other 15 f-variants ARE msvcrt exports (runtime-witnessed) → ungated rows. SURFACE (D-CSUBSET-TGMATH-SURFACE): the C23 7.25 names NOT in math.json (frexp/modf/round/trunc/hypot/cbrt/exp2/log2/lround/remainder/…, the complex cabs/csqrt/… routing, and the C23 _Decimal names) are NOT shipped and fail LOUD by absence (S_UndeclaredIdentifier on use); <tgmath.h> nominally includes <math.h>+<complex.h> (7.25p1) — a consumer needing INFINITY/I includes those headers directly (same -SURFACE row).

macros.name: pow
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), float: _Generic((y), float: powf((float)(x), (float)(y)), default: pow((x), (y))), default: pow((x), (y)))

macros.name: atan2
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), float: _Generic((y), float: atan2f((float)(x), (float)(y)), default: atan2((x), (y))), default: atan2((x), (y)))

macros.name: fmod
macros.replacement: _Generic((x), float: _Generic((y), float: fmodf((float)(x), (float)(y)), default: fmod((x), (y))), default: fmod((x), (y)))
